collaborate.definition: To work together with others to achieve a common goal.

collective.definition: A farm owned by a collection of people

colonial.definition: A person from a country that is or was controlled by another.

columnist.definition: A regular writer of a column, such as in a magazine or newspaper

combat.definition: A battle, a fight (often one in which weapons are used).

commentary.definition: A series of comments or annotations; especially, a book of explanations or expositions on the whole or a part of some other work

commentator.definition: A person who comments; especially someone who is paid to give his/her opinions in the media about current affairs, sports, etc.

commerce.definition: The exchange or buying and selling of commodities; especially the exchange of merchandise, on a large scale, between different places or communities; extended trade or traffic.

commodity.definition: Anything movable (a good) that is bought and sold.

communist.definition: An advocate of a society based on the common ownership of property; a proponent of communism.

companion.definition: A friend, acquaintance, or partner; someone with whom one spends time or keeps company

compassion.definition: Deep awareness of the suffering of another, coupled with the wish to relieve it.

compensate.definition: To do (something good) after (something bad) happens

compensation.definition: The act or principle of compensating.

competence.definition: The quality or state of being competent, i.e. able or suitable for a general role.

competent.definition: Having sufficient skill, knowledge, ability, or qualifications.

complement.definition: A protective substance that exists in the serum or other bodily fluid and is capable of killing microorganisms; complement.

complexity.definition: The state of being complex; intricacy; entanglement.

complication.definition: The act or process of complicating.

comply.definition: To yield assent; to accord; to acquiesce, agree, consent; to adapt oneself, to conform.

composition.definition: The act of putting together; assembly.

compromise.definition: The settlement of differences by arbitration or by consent reached by mutual concessions.

conceal.definition: To hide something from view or from public knowledge, to try to keep something secret.

concede.definition: To yield or suffer; to surrender; to grant

conceive.definition: To develop an idea; to form in the mind; to plan; to devise; to originate.

condemn.definition: To strongly criticise or denounce; to excoriate the perpetrators of.

confer.definition: To grant as a possession; to bestow.

confession.definition: The open admittance of having done something (especially something bad).

configuration.definition: Form, as depending on the relative disposition of the parts of a thing's shape; figure; form factor.

confine.definition: (chiefly in the plural) A boundary or limit.

confirmation.definition: An official indicator that things will happen as planned.

confront.definition: To stand or meet facing, especially in competition, hostility or defiance; to come face to face with

confrontation.definition: The act of confronting or challenging another, especially face to face.

congratulate.definition: To express one’s sympathetic pleasure or joy to the person(s) it is felt for.

congregation.definition: The act of congregating or collecting together.

congressional.definition: Of or pertaining to a congress.

conquer.definition: To defeat in combat; to subjugate.

conscience.definition: The moral sense of right and wrong, chiefly as it affects one's own behaviour.

consciousness.definition: The state of being conscious or aware; awareness.

consecutive.definition: A sequence of notes or chords that results from repeated shifts in pitch of the same interval.

consensus.definition: A process of decision-making that seeks widespread agreement among group members.

conserve.definition: Wilderness where human development is prohibited.

consolidate.definition: To combine into a single unit; to group together or join.

constitution.definition: The act, or process of setting something up, or establishing something; the composition or structure of such a thing; its makeup.

constitutional.definition: A walk that is taken regularly for good health and wellbeing.

contemplate.definition: To look at on all sides or in all its aspects; to view or consider with continued attention; to regard with deliberate care; to meditate on; to study, ponder, or consider.

contempt.definition: The state or act of contemning; the feeling or attitude of regarding someone or something as inferior, base, or worthless; scorn, disdain.

contend.definition: To strive in opposition; to contest; to dispute; to vie; to quarrel; to fight.

contender.definition: Someone who competes with one or more other people.

contractor.definition: A person or company that builds or improves buildings.

contributor.definition: A benefactor; someone who donates to charity or some cause.

conversion.definition: The act of converting something or someone.

convict.definition: A person convicted of a crime by a judicial body.

cooperate.definition: To work or act together, especially for a common purpose or benefit.

cooperative.definition: A type of company that is owned partially or wholly by its employees, customers or tenants.

coordinate.definition: A number representing the position of a point along a line, arc, or similar one-dimensional figure.

coordination.definition: The act of coordinating, making different people or things work together for a goal or effect.

copper.definition: A reddish-brown, malleable, ductile metallic element with high electrical and thermal conductivity, symbol Cu, and atomic number 29.

copyright.definition: The right by law to be the entity which determines who may publish, copy and distribute a piece of writing, music, picture or other work of authorship.

correlate.definition: Either of a pair of things related by a correlation; a correlative.

correlation.definition: A reciprocal, parallel or complementary relationship between two or more comparable objects.

correspond.definition: (constructed with to) To be equivalent or similar in character, quantity, quality, origin, structure, function etc.

correspondent.definition: Someone who or something which corresponds.

corresponding.definition: (constructed with to) To be equivalent or similar in character, quantity, quality, origin, structure, function etc.

corrupt.definition: To make corrupt; to change from good to bad; to draw away from the right path; to deprave; to pervert.

corruption.definition: The act of corrupting or of impairing integrity, virtue, or moral principle; the state of being corrupted or debased; loss of purity or integrity

counselling.definition: To give advice, especially professional advice, to (somebody).

counsellor.definition: A professional who counsels people, especially on personal problems.

counterpart.definition: Either of two parts that fit together, or complement one another.

countless.definition: Too many to count; innumerable or incalculable

coup.definition: A quick, brilliant, and highly successful act.
